title: tephrosia rugelii
intro: Tephrosia rugelii, also known as wild horsefly or buffalo clover, is a perennial legume native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and wounds due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves and roots are rich in antioxidants, which may contribute to its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Tephrosia rugelii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
